meta.name: PRMQ Prospective and Retrospective Memory Questionnaire
meta.description: The PRMQ is a 16-item self-report measure that assesses the frequency of everyday memory failures. It distinguishes between prospective memory (remembering to do things in the future) and retrospective memory (recalling past events and information).
meta.author: Smith et al. (2000)
meta.reference: Smith G, Della Sala S, Logie RH, Maylor EA. Prospective and retrospective memory in normal ageing and dementia: a questionnaire study. Mem Cognit. 2000;28(6):881-889.
meta.instruction: The following questions are about the kinds of memory mistakes that everyone makes from time to time. Please indicate how often each of these things happens to you.

questions.text: Do you decide to do something in a few minutes' time and then forget to do it?
Do you fail to recognize a place you have visited before?
Do you fail to do something you were supposed to do a few minutes later even though it's there in front of you?
Do you forget something that you were told a few minutes before?
Do you forget appointments if you are not prompted by someone else or by a reminder?
Do you fail to recognize a character in a radio or television show from scene to scene?
Do you forget to buy something you planned to buy, like a birthday card, even when you see the shop?
Do you fail to recall things that have happened to you in the last few days?
Do you repeat the same story to the same person on different occasions?
Do you intend to take something with you but minutes later leave it behind?
Do you mislay something that you have just put down, like a magazine or glasses?
Do you fail to mention or give something to a visitor that you were asked to pass on?
Do you look at something without realizing you have seen it moments before?
If you tried to contact a friend or relative who was out, would you forget to try again later?
Do you forget what you watched on television the previous day?
Do you forget to tell someone something you had meant to mention a few minutes ago?

ranges.label: Good Memory
ranges.description: Your overall memory performance appears to be good, with infrequent memory failures in daily life. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your current memory strategies
Keep up mentally stimulating activities
Maintain healthy lifestyle habits that support cognitive function
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences

ranges.label: Average Memory
ranges.description: Your memory performance is within the average range. You experience occasional memory lapses that are common in daily life. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Use external aids like calendars and reminder apps for important tasks
Establish consistent routines to support prospective memory
Stay mentally active through reading, puzzles, or learning new skills
Ensure adequate sleep, as it is critical for memory consolidation
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise

ranges.label: Poor Memory
ranges.description: Your score suggests you experience memory failures more frequently than average. This may affect daily functioning. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Implement systematic reminder strategies (phone alarms, sticky notes, to-do lists)
Minimize distractions when trying to remember important information
Ensure adequate sleep, as sleep is critical for memory consolidation
Consider whether stress or multitasking may be contributing to memory lapses
Consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: Very Poor Memory
ranges.description: Your score indicates frequent memory failures that may significantly impact daily life. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Consider consulting a healthcare professional for a thorough memory assessment
Use multiple external memory aids consistently
Simplify routines and reduce cognitive load where possible
Discuss with a professional whether underlying factors may be involved
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible

prospective.label: Good Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You generally remember to carry out planned actions and future intentions. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

prospective.label: Average Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You occasionally forget to carry out planned actions, which is within the normal range. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

prospective.label: Poor Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You frequently forget to carry out intended actions. Consider using external reminders. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

prospective.label: Very Poor Prospective Memory
prospective.description: You very frequently forget planned actions. Systematic use of external aids is strongly recommended. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support

retrospective.label: Good Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You generally recall past events and previously learned information well. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

retrospective.label: Average Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You occasionally have difficulty recalling past events, which is within the normal range. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

retrospective.label: Poor Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You frequently have difficulty remembering past events and information. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

retrospective.label: Very Poor Retrospective Memory
retrospective.description: You very frequently have difficulty recalling past events. Consider seeking professional assessment. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible
Make sure you have trusted people around you for support
Follow the treatment plan recommended by your professional
Maintain basic daily self-care routines
If you feel overwhelmed, call a mental health helpline for immediate support
